channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Messi and Argentina survive another close call to reach the World Cup semifinals
item.description: Argentina was taken to the brink in its first two knockout games. In Saturday's quarterfinal against Switzerland, the Albiceleste survived again to advance to the semis, where they will face England.
item.content:encoded:
Argentina was taken to the brink in its first two knockout games. In Saturday's quarterfinal against Switzerland, the Albiceleste survived again to advance to the semis, where they will face England.

(Image credit: Odd Andersen)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: 200 young campers rescued as flooding hits parts of Missouri and Kentucky
item.description: A historic rainfall event has left communities across several Missouri counties underwater and prompted water rescues, including an evacuation of a summer camp.
item.content:encoded: A historic rainfall event has left communities across several Missouri counties underwater and prompted water rescues, including an evacuation of a summer camp.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: In a nailbiter, England moves on to the World Cup semifinals, defeating Norway 2-1
item.description: England's Jude Bellingham has done it again. Scoring both of his team's goals in a thrilling quarterfinal against Norway that needed extra time. It was the first World Cup meeting between the two.
item.content:encoded:
England's Jude Bellingham has done it again. Scoring both of his team's goals in a thrilling quarterfinal against Norway that needed extra time. It was the first World Cup meeting between the two.

(Image credit: Richard Pelham)
item.dc:creator: Russell Lewis

item.title: Justice Department subpoenas New York Times reporters over Air Force One reporting
item.description: The Times says federal agents turned up on the doorsteps of several of its journalists to force grand jury testimony next week over their coverage of the Air Force One plane gifted to Trump by Qatar.
item.content:encoded:
The Times says federal agents turned up on the doorsteps of several of its journalists to force grand jury testimony next week over their coverage of the Air Force One plane gifted to Trump by Qatar.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: The biggest steam locomotive is whistle-stopping across the U.S.
item.description: Huge crowds of train fans turn out as the 1940s era Big Boy steam locomotive is making a rare trip cross country.
item.content:encoded:
Huge crowds of train fans turn out as the 1940s era Big Boy steam locomotive is making a rare trip cross country.

(Image credit: Michelle Gustafson for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jeff Lunden

item.title: With the white nationalist group Patriot Front, what you see is not what you get
item.description: Hundreds of masked white nationalists marched in the nation's capital on July Fourth. Who were they and where does their funding come from?
item.content:encoded:
Hundreds of masked white nationalists marched in the nation's capital on July Fourth. Who were they and where does their funding come from?

(Image credit: Cheney Orr)
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef

item.title: Trump administration rolls back a key protection for imperiled wildlife
item.description: The Trump administration finalized a rule Friday that changes how agencies enforce the Endangered Species Act. The administration narrowed the definition of "harm" under the landmark law.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration finalized a rule Friday that changes how agencies enforce the Endangered Species Act. The administration narrowed the definition of "harm" under the landmark law.

(Image credit: Carolyn Kaster)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Poor counties adjust to cuts in food stamp program
item.description: Poor counties in the South are bracing for steep cuts in administering food aid as President Trump's tax and spending bill brings new requirements.
item.content:encoded: Poor counties in the South are bracing for steep cuts in administering food aid as President Trump's tax and spending bill brings new requirements.
item.dc:creator: Nick de la Canal

item.title: Graham Platner makes it official in Maine, submitting paperwork to leave Senate race
item.description: In his withdrawal notice, Platner said "people are desperate for change" and that's why they made him the Democratic nominee. Now, Maine Democrats have to pick someone to replace him by July 27.
item.content:encoded:
In his withdrawal notice, Platner said "people are desperate for change" and that's why they made him the Democratic nominee. Now, Maine Democrats have to pick someone to replace him by July 27.

(Image credit: CJ Gunther)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: Utah judge now considering evidence against accused killer of Charlie Kirk
item.description: A weeklong hearing to establish whether the accused killer of conservative activist Charlie Kirk can be taken to full trial ended today. There has been dramatic testimony.
item.content:encoded: A weeklong hearing to establish whether the accused killer of conservative activist Charlie Kirk can be taken to full trial ended today. There has been dramatic testimony.
item.dc:creator: Sean Higgins
